body.title: Clan: global vs. instance-local knowledge scoping
body.result: C-clan-instance-scoping concept node; bus/memory_protocol.py typed schemas; 9 tests; bus-not-DB invariant verified
body.description:
Define the boundary between global clan knowledge and instance-local knowledge. A UU instance running someone's legal citation project should not receive our sprint ticket history, and we should not receive their domain patterns — but both instances can benefit from shared universal patterns (how to structure a ticket, common coding idioms, agent bootstrap sequences).

Global clan knowledge: universal patterns, agent bootstrap templates, Igor-template baseline memories. Instance-local: our sprint outcomes, our project patterns, our Akien/Leah client data. The clan IS the global layer — joining the clan means sharing global, not local.

Also defines the inter-agent knowledge request protocol: an agent asking another agent for access to agent-specific data sends a structured bus request; the owning agent approves or denies. This is distinct from the access control model in T-memory-scope-layers (which handles the DB-level isolation).

**Affected files:** TBD — palace concept node (C-clan-instance-scoping); skeleton or bus layer for inter-agent request protocol

**Design rules:** palace-first for concept; bus+shim for protocol; no direct cross-agent DB access

**Scope boundary:** IN — global vs. local boundary definition; clan join protocol; inter-agent knowledge request protocol (bus-native). OUT — actual global DB population; encryption; human-facing access management UI

**Completion criteria:** (1) palace concept node defines global/local/agent/client boundary; (2) inter-agent knowledge request has a typed bus message schema; (3) a test agent requesting Igor's memories gets a bus response, not a direct DB query
